title: Create Data Stream
content:
# Create Data Stream

Create a new Data Stream.

## Endpoint

`POST /datastreams/create`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.datastreams.createDataStream()
print(response)

description.example: The rate at which gas flows from the reservoir to the surface.
description.description: Detailed description of the new Data Stream.

name.description: Unique identifier `name` for the new Data Stream. Can only contain lowercase alphanumeric characters and `.`, `_` or `-` characters.

semantic_type_name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Semantic Type that describes the nature, purpose or origin of the data.

title.description: Display name (`title`) of the Data Stream.

type.description: Specifies the technique used for generating data in the Data Stream.

unit_name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Units that describes the type or category of data represented by each unit.

created.description: UTC time when the Data Stream was first created, formatted in RFC 3339.

description.example: The rate at which gas flows from the reservoir to the surface.
description.description: Detailed description of the Data Stream.

name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Data Stream.

semantic_type_name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Semantic Type that describes the nature, purpose or origin of the data.

title.description: Display name (`title`) of the Data Stream.

type.description: Specifies the technique used for generating data in the Data Stream.

unit_name.description: Unique identifier `name` of the Units that describes the type or category of data represented by each unit. Only available if the Primitive Type is `number`.

is_protected.description: Indicates whether the Data Stream is protected or not. If `true`, the Data Stream is protected and cannot be deleted or modified.

in_use_by.description: List of workload sources that are using this Data Stream.

updated.description: UTC time when any Data Stream keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.
